subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF, OECD 2026 Survey, World Justice Project, RSF, EEA, World Weather Attribution) + Portuguese institutions (INE, Banco de Portugal, used as independent data) + EU/Iberian press.

Source-lean: the physical/health factors leaned on alarm-toned Anglophone/expat press, partly corrected with EEA/WWA/ESA and Portuguese official data; Portuguese-language press (Público/Expresso/Observador) was not read directly this pass.
Climate is the standout negative and worsens with horizon (2025 megafire season; Feb 2026 record storms).

economic.notes: A sound and improving fiscal position within the eurozone, well-capitalized banks, and diversified services/tourism exports; near-term energy-shock inflation the main wobble.

fiscal_state.notes: Budget surpluses 2022-24; debt fell to ~89.7% GDP (2025) and still declining; slips to a small deficit as the RRF tapers; long-term pension/health pressure from ageing. (OECD 2026, IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB sets policy and is credible and independent; no national monetary control. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Was converging to ~2.1%; spiked to ~3.3% YoY (Apr 2026) on an energy shock; expected back to target long-term. (Banco de Portugal, EC)

banking_stability.notes: CET1 ~17.9%, NPL ~2.3%, strong 2025 profitability; countercyclical buffer 0.75% from Jan 2026. (Banco de Portugal FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified EU-delegated trade; goods deficit offset by tourism/services; large undeveloped lithium reserves (upside); no resource curse; mild near-term drag from energy/storm shocks. (OECD, OEC)

institutional.notes: Independent judiciary that has actively checked the executive, uncompromised official statistics, and high press freedom; civil-service trust the soft spot.

rule_of_law.notes: Independent judiciary (WJP ~29/143); the Constitutional Court struck down key 2025 immigration clauses - a live check on the executive; persistent court-delay weakness. (WJP, EU Rule of Law Report)

statistical_integrity.notes: INE is independent and ESS-compliant; uncompromised. (INE, Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Professional with strong digital government, but low public trust (~40%) and an ageing workforce. (OECD Government at a Glance 2025)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 8th/180 (2025), 'satisfactory'; pluralistic media with some economic-precarity pressure. (RSF)

political_social.notes: Strong civil liberties and a favorable, lawful residency pathway, against a minority government amid a populist surge and an ageing population.

political_stability.notes: AD won the May 2025 snap election but governs as a minority; the hard-right Chega surged to ~23% (now the second party); recurring budget fragility, absorbed constitutionally. (IDEA, Atlantic Council)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong protections; a functioning Constitutional Court; no notable rollback. (WJP, EU report)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Favorable residency pathways (D7 passive-income, D8 digital-nomad, citizenship eligibility ~5y); a 2025 law tightened family reunification and the Golden Visa real-estate route ended, but the Constitutional Court overturned the harshest clauses, keeping the pathway open and lawful (AIMA processing backlog the main friction). (Constitutional Court; Lexology)

social_cohesion.notes: Historically cohesive and low-polarization, but rapid immigration and Chega's rise are straining it; no major unrest. (IDEA)

demographics.notes: TFR ~1.40, median age ~47, heavy ageing; net migration (>1M foreign residents) currently offsets decline, but the central projection falls toward ~8.3M by 2100. (INE 2025)

geopolitical.notes: Low conflict exposure on the Atlantic flank; reliable if small NATO/EU member; no capital controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO founding member and committed EU member; hit 2% GDP defense (2025), pledged 3.5% by 2035; reliable though a small contributor. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: Atlantic-flank geography, no border threats, no active conflict; supports Ukraine via EU/NATO. (NATO)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions-regime participant; no domestic capital controls (crisis-era 2010-12 controls long resolved). (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Low crime and decent infrastructure, but a worsening climate profile and a strained health service; high latent seismic risk.

climate.notes: Iberian heat, severe drought/desertification (south), and major wildfire, lower readiness than core EU. (IPCC AR6; EEA; Germanwatch CRI)

healthcare.notes: Universal SNS free at point of use, but a large operating deficit, ER/maternity closures, doctor strikes, and falling public trust; reform pressure mounting. (FNAM; Portuguese press)

infrastructure.notes: Generally good transport and broadband; the 2026 storms exposed planning gaps with repairs running into late 2026. (Euronews)

crime_safety.notes: Low violent crime (Global Peace Index 7th, 2025); main risk is petty theft in Lisbon/Porto. (GPI; RASI)

disaster_insurance.notes: High latent seismic risk (much of Lisbon predates anti-seismic code; only ~19% of homes carry quake cover) plus newly realized flood exposure; functional but underinsured. (Verisk; APS)

summary: Strong fundamentals - a sound and improving fiscal position within the eurozone, well-capitalized banks, top-tier press freedom, and an independent judiciary that has actively checked the executive - paired with favorable, lawful residency pathways. The drags are a minority government amid a hard-right surge, a strained public health service, an ageing population only offset by immigration, and a worsening climate profile (record 2025 wildfires and a severe 2026 storm season) atop high latent seismic risk.
